Cybersecurity is protecting computers, networks, and data from attacks, damage, or unauthorized access. It includes firewalls, encryption, password protection, and security awareness. It is like putting locks, alarms, and guards around your digital information.
Cybersecurity can feel hard at the beginning, but it becomes easier when you take it step by step and keep practicing. Feeling confused at first is normal, not a sign that you are weak.

I can help with: registration info, admission requirements, deadlines, fees, scholarships, study tips, cybersecurity basics, why cybersecurity matters, Python installation and basics, Git and GitHub commands, how to push code to GitHub, course structure, assessments, grading, and general school questions. Just ask in simple English and I'll do my best!

Common Git commands:
git init  → start a repo
git status  → see changes
git add .  → stage changes
git commit -m "msg"  → save snapshot
git log  → see history
git remote add origin URL  → link to GitHub
git push -u origin main  → push
git pull  → get latest
git clone URL  → copy a repo
git add stages your changes for commit. Example: git add file.py or git add . to stage all changed files. Think of it as marking what you want to include in your next snapshot.
git status shows which files are changed, staged, or untracked in your working directory. Run it often to stay aware of your project state.
